findings.issue: Smoke greps verify the SystemForegroundService declaration exists (TAC-8) but do not verify its critical attributes: android:foregroundServiceType="dataSync" and tools:node="merge". A wrong foregroundServiceType slips past compileDebugKotlin and past the current grep, and surfaces only as a runtime crash on Android 14+ when WorkManager attempts to start the foreground service (SecurityException: Starting FGS with type dataSync ... callerTargetSdkVersion=34 requires permissions: ...). A missing tools:node="merge" produces a manifest-merger collision with the WorkManager library manifest which would be caught by the build, but the attribute pair is the real contract here — verify both.
findings.fix:
Add to Verification Steps → Smoke:
- grep -E 'foregroundServiceType="dataSync"' app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml → ≥ 1 match.
- grep -E 'tools:node="merge"' app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml → ≥ 1 match.
Both are one-liners and close the silent-failure gap that on-device QA would only catch once the first download is kicked off.

findings.issue: Smoke greps do not verify the MainActivity declaration has android:exported="true" with the MAIN/LAUNCHER intent-filter, nor that <application android:name=".SanctumApplication"> is wired. These are acceptance criteria but are not structurally asserted. If the exported flag, intent-filter, or application name is wrong or missing, compileDebugKotlin still succeeds; the failure shows up only as "app won't launch from launcher" or "Hilt fails to initialize" on first install — exactly the kind of silent break the smoke layer should catch before handoff to manual QA.
findings.fix:
Add to Verification Steps → Smoke:
- grep -E 'android:name="\.MainActivity"' app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml → ≥ 1 match.
- grep -E 'android.intent.action.MAIN' app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml → ≥ 1 match.
- grep -E 'android.intent.category.LAUNCHER' app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml → ≥ 1 match.
- grep -E 'android:name="\.SanctumApplication"' app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml → ≥ 1 match.

findings.issue: strings.xml canonical-key check verifies names and count, but does not verify that the four format strings actually contain Android positional placeholders. If a future edit rewrites model_size_gb_format to '%.1f ГБ' (dropping the positional form), Task 8 will crash at runtime with MissingFormatArgumentException the first time getString(R.string.model_size_gb_format, sizeGb) is called — and the existing greps will still pass. Task 7 is the owner of strings.xml contract for Phase 1, so this is worth pinning here rather than deferring to Task 8.
findings.fix:
Add to Verification Steps → Smoke:
- grep -E 'model_size_gb_format">%1\$\.1f' app/src/main/res/values/strings.xml → ≥ 1 match.
- grep -E 'model_download_progress_format">%1\$d / %2\$d' app/src/main/res/values/strings.xml → ≥ 1 match.
- grep -E 'model_error_prefix">.*%1\$s' app/src/main/res/values/strings.xml → ≥ 1 match.
- grep -E 'ttft_footer_format">.*%1\$d.*%2\$\.1f' app/src/main/res/values/strings.xml → ≥ 1 match.

findings.issue: themes.xml uses parent="android:Theme.Material.Light.NoActionBar" (platform Material 1 / Holo-era) rather than the Material 3 parent the task hint suggested (Theme.Material3.DayNight.NoActionBar). This is a strategy/quality observation rather than a test gap: the theme is only used for the pre-Compose splash frame, so it builds and launches fine, but it contradicts the task guidance and the dynamic-color branch in SanctumTheme. Not a test-reviewer blocker, flagging for visibility so downstream tasks (Task 10 onwards) aren't surprised by a dark-mode splash regression.
findings.fix: Either update themes.xml parent to Theme.Material3.DayNight.NoActionBar (requires com.google.android.material dependency in :app), or note in decisions.md that the platform Material.Light parent is intentional for the pre-Compose handoff frame and dark-mode handling happens inside SanctumTheme.

summary: Approve. D8's scoping out of unit tests for this task is correct and consistent with test-master guidance that mobile-app framework glue (Application class, Activity permission request, Compose theme wrapper, declarative manifest, strings.xml) belongs in the E2E / on-device layer rather than the unit layer — unit-testing any of this code path would require mocking 3+ Android framework dependencies, which the test-master rule explicitly calls out as "wrong test type." The smoke grep set covers the high-risk TAC targets (TAC-7 permissions, TAC-12 hardening flags, TAC-14 FQN plumbing, canonical 22-key strings.xml contract) well. Three medium/low smoke-grep gaps remain — foregroundServiceType+tools:node on the FGS, MainActivity exported+intent-filter+application name wiring, and format-placeholder preservation in strings.xml — all cheap to close and all protect against changes that compileDebugKotlin would not catch. None of these rise to 'requires a unit test'; they are additional one-line greps at the same verification tier. No unit test is justified for this task.
